evidence.label: late board
evidence.value: Poppy slot 4 | Meepsie slot 3 | Aurora slot 22 | Illaoi slot 2 | Master Yi slot 7 with Edge of Night, Giant Slayer, Target-Lock Optics | Rammus slot 6 with Evenshroud, Spirit Visage, Steadfast Heart | Blitzcrank slot 1 | Sona slot 28 with Spear of Shojin, Morellonomicon | Fiora slot 5 with Sterak's Gage, Bloodthirster, Quicksilver

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Best played with Soraka +HP God blessings. Situational Fast 9 comp you pivot to when you hit Bronze For Life or have reason to play many synergies. Ideally played with a strong Anima lose streak cash out. If no Bronze for Life, drop Poppy for Shen.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Ideally open with Anima to begin lose streak. Be careful about slamming items and being too strong. Position poorly if needed to guarantee losses.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Push the limit and go for 500+ cash out because you can get Fiora 2 with items. BUT make sure to have at least 2 lives. If you need time to transition, roll during PvE rounds.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: If successful, you should be so strong on Stage 4, you don't need to roll much and can push directly to 9. However, be careful because level 8 players may take your 4 cost tanks.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Positioning is key to get the middle of the board open for Pyke to jump on enemy carries. Good with extra team slots to play more Voyagers. Can also play Viktor as duo carry if your Psionic item is good for him (Drone > Implant). Rhaast and Nami can go in on at 8.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Get Meepsie online with items ASAP and try to win streak if possible. If lose streak, you can look for an econ engine like Primordian / Timebreaker.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Level to 6 and roll for 2* Meepsie and activate her traits (Meeple > Voyager > Shepherd priority). Econ back up to 50g and slow roll for 3* Meepsie.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Finish Meepsie + Pyke 3* and push levels. Ideally tank Morde 3 but Illaoi 2 is fine temporarily. Level for Voyagers / Rhaast.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Always aim for 6 Vanguard. If you can't hit Teemo 3, push levels for Leblanc instead. Preferably choose a tanky Arbiter trait. If you have Space Groove emblem, you can drop Leblanc for Nami.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Prioritize Nasus BIS and aim for a win streak if possible while farming kills for his permanent scaling. Aim for a Vanguard / Space Groove mix of units.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Slow roll for Nasus 3. Leona 3 and Teemo 3 are luxury. If you highroll Leblanc early and are behind in Teemo count, consider carrying her instead.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Finish your 3* and push levels for 6 Vanguard. You can flex in 5 Space Groove, Zoe, or a support 5 cost until you hit Blitzcrank.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Only play this with Bronze for Life. Look for Bard + Rammus 2 on 8 or going fast 9 with natural Leblanc. You can also drop Leblanc for Sona if you don't have attack speed items. However, even without Arbiter Leblanc is our best carry. If you don't have BFL, play Shen over Ornn.

evidence.value: Play around early Arbiters with Zoe as the item holder. You can also play around Teemo and Nasus too. Slam items for Leblanc > Tank.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Look for 4 Vanguard and 3 Shepherd as your core. Leblanc 1 is strong and can immediately be played if you high roll her early.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level to 8 and roll for Leblanc Karma and frontline. If you hit Nunu 2 with good Arbiter play Vanguard version until you can find Rammus 2 and Bard. Rhaast on 9,

evidence.label: Augment note

evidence.value: Play a standard win streak tempo opener. You can't aim to play this comp unless you specifically hit Zed's augments.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Build Zed items ASAP: Edge of Night > Shojin. Slam items on anyone to preserve HP while focusing on econ. We will be rolling hard on level 8.

evidence.value: Level to 8 and look for a 4 cost tank. Zed has no traits so be very flexible with front line / back line. Some ideas: 4 Vanguard + Zed, 5 NOVA + Zed, or any comp that plays Riven / Yi.

comps.title: Reach for the Stars Jax Reroll (TFT Academy)

evidence.label: Provider title
evidence.value: Reach for the Stars Jax Reroll

evidence.label: early board
evidence.value: Aatrox | Talon | Twisted Fate | Jax with Guinsoo's Rageblade, Bloodthirster, Titan's Resolve

evidence.label: late board
evidence.value: Talon 3-star slot 5 | Caitlyn 3-star slot 26 with Guinsoo's Rageblade, Kraken's Fury | Aatrox 3-star slot 6 with Sunfire Cape, Dragon's Claw, Bramble Vest | Twisted Fate 3-star slot 28 with Nashor's Tooth, Nashor's Tooth, Rabadon's Deathcap | Gwen 3-star slot 19 | Jax 3-star slot 13 with Guinsoo's Rageblade, Titan's Resolve, Bloodthirster | Rhaast slot 4

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Best on 3* buff Stargazer Constellation. Take lots of item and econ augments to hit the board. Many games you won't be able to fully itemize both TF and Caitlyn in the back so only prioritize one of them.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Get Jax online with items ASAP and try to win streak if possible. If lose streak, you can look for an econ engine like Primordian / Timebreaker.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Level to 6 and roll for 2* Jax and the Stargazer NOVA board. Econ back up to 50g and slow roll for 3* Jax. Finish any 3* you can, but often you can't chase all of them at the same time.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: If playing on Medallion Stargazer you could justify staying lower level to hit other things like Gwen 3 / Talon 3. Rhaast goes in on 7. Can also play 4 Bastion on 8.

lines: Finish Gragas and push levels. Ideally tank Tahm 2 but you can use any upgraded Brawler. Cap with 6 Brawler / 4 Psionic.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Don't reroll on low levels. Instead, use the 2 star to tempo win streak and finish printing the 3 star with 7 meeple -> go fast 9 and 10. Board shown is only the end game cap so it's important to flex Stage 4 with units like Mighty Mech + Meepsie.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Get Poppy online with items ASAP and try to win streak if possible. We are getting poppy 3 with 7 meeple print later so just push levels as standard.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Level for 5 Meeple + Bastions. Hold at least 1 copy of Meeple so you can get in 7 ASAP. It is worth leveling early (Stage 3-5) for 7 Meeple if you have it.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level to 8 and roll for 1 Rammus and Corki to get 7 Meeple. Play Mighty Mech + Meepsie on 8 before you hit Bard. Blitz Morde on 9. Rhaast on 10.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Best with Arbiter that scales Leblanc's AP. Late game, if your Arbiter is not good cap with Blitzcrank over Leona. Comp can use strong frontline emblems such as Vanguard, Voyager, or Arbiter Emblems. Play Mighty mech over Karma on level 8 if you find sona for 7 Shepherd, frontline is most important.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Play around early Arbiter opener, preferably one that scales AP. If you take econ Arbiter, ditch it late game for a better tank option. Always take Frying Pan when possible.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Level for 5 Shepherd when possible with Vanguards. Focus on econ management while preserving HP because we will roll heavily on level 8.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level and roll for 5 Shepherd + Vanguard frontline. If you don't hit Sona and you have strong AP Arbiter, keep Zoe in. Otherwise, flex in a duo carry like Karma or Bard.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Tempo composition that is best played from ahead. Best Psionic item = Malware Matrix on Yi. Related trait emblems are good: Marauder > Brawler = Psionic > Challenger.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Best played from high tempo + econ engine like Primordian or Timebreaker. Slam items on Bel'Veth or Briar as an item holder.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Standard level 6 and 7 intervals (3-2 / 3-5 if streaking). Play around 4 Brawler + Marauder + NOVA. Master Yi 1 can immediately go in with items.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level to 8 at a natural interval and roll until Master Yi 2 with upgraded Brawlers. Kindred takes leftover bow items. Fiora takes leftover fighter items.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Riven is a common 4 cost but you can be flexible with other units (Xayah, Leblanc, 5 costs). End game, move items from Kaisa 2 -> Jhin 2 and Karma 2. Emblems are good: Voyager, Rogue, Tank, or Sniper emblems are strong.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Look for an econ engine like Primordian or Timebreaker that fit in Dark Star naturally. Slam items for Karma / Jhin and push levels.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level to 8 and roll down for 6 Dark Star. Always play Jhin. Illaoi 2 is the cheapest tank to let you push levels but you can also tank Mighty Mech / Nunu / TK.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Prioritize items on Briar > Bel'Veth / Kai'Sa. Positioning shown is for most of early and mid game, but late game you can play a standard default melee carry positioning. Rhaast on 8, Jhin on 9. Tahm 2 is a better tank than Reksai 3.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Field 3 Primordian and start collecting Primordians. Early game you can solo frontline Briar in top corner when you have Rogue activated.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Slow roll on 5 for Primordian units and Cho 3 if very rich. Finish items for Briar, Rek'Sai, and Bel'Veth. If Briar solo frontline is falling off, change to default melee positioning.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Finish your 3* then push levels for Rhaast / Kindred. Kai'sa takes any leftover items (can also hold Sunder / Anti-heal items). If no Cho 3, cap with Jhin instead.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Only Play with the Mountain Stargazer constellation. The Riven + Morde + Jhin spots are highly flexible so play around whatever upgrades you can hit to get to level 9 for 5 costs. When capping the board, take out weak, low cost Stargazers: TF > Lulu / Talon.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Always keep at least 3 Stargazer in for the Emblem. Look for a Rageblade start on Twisted Fate 2 or Riven items on Talon 2.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Add more Stargazers as you level. Tech in Timebreaker when you can. The 1 cost carries fall off by end of Stage 3 so expect losses or transition to higher tier carries.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level to 8 and roll for Riven, Nunu, and Xayah. Always play Shen. Once you hit at least 2 of the 3 units, you can consider pushing to 9.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Only S tier with Boar and Serpent Stargazers. Altar and Mountain are A tier if you have them on 2-1. Medallion is C tier. Fountain is completely unplayable. Anima cash out weapon Battle Bunny Crossbow + Rageblade + IE = easy first.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Play for win streak, ideally with Timebreaker but that will be rare because units are weaker on average. Slam items aggressively.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: If win streaking, prioritize playing strongest board over greeding econ traits. Upgraded 2 and 3 costs are often better than 1* high tier units here.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Roll for Xayah and Nunu 2. Play Samira with Rhaast before you find Jhin to help you go 9. Rammus isn't that good without bard so he's a lower prio upgrade.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 8. Roll for desired comp until stable. "Stable" changes depending on rank, items, augments, composition, and patch. Melee carries and tanks get the biggest spike for 2-stars. Usually stable involves 2-star tank or carry and some other supporting 2-stars. Try not to roll below 10g.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you are weak (e.g., have no 2-star 4-costs), consider rolling again to 10g here. If you are stable, save gold to go 9.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you are low HP and haven't 2-starred your carry and tank, roll to 0g. If you have medium to high HP with an upgraded + itemized 4-cost unit and some other upgrades, save gold to go 9.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If high HP, consider waiting until 5-5 to go level 9. If medium HP, consider going level 9 if you can roll 20g or wait until 5-5. If low HP and can afford to go 9, do so even if you have almost no gold to roll on 9. If you can't afford to go 9 this game, roll every turn for upgrades.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 9 if possible. From this point onward, look to level to 9. Acknowledge games where level 9 is not possible and roll every turn on 8 to preserve placements.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: For max cap games, you can either roll on 9 or level to 10. If you are itemizing a 5-cost carry and it is 1-starred, roll on 9. If you have all your main itemized units upgraded, level to 10.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: This guide used to be called "Fast 9 Graves", but that is a misnomer as Graves is not always played or mandatory for this comp.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Duo carry can be Graves or Fiora. Ideally it is Graves, but if you find him late then consider playing for Fiora instead. This is part of why you should consider melee items over Power Frame or Marksman Frame items earlier in the game as they can flexibly go on Fiora.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Play for Graves if high enough HP and hit him early enough. Otherwise can flex Jhin or Rhaast.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: All 3 Graves forms are viable, so play flexibly based on what items you have.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: The most important use of Glove in this line is Thief's Gloves (TG). Salvage Bin is good because it allows you to slam Glove items and then eventually remake them into TGs later on.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: In an ideal world, play for this line when you can winstreak Stage 2 and Stage 3 and then play Timebreaker while saving gold in Stage 4, spike on either 4-5 or 5-1 depending on gold / HP.

evidence.value: Without econ augment, fast 9 is almost impossible without win-streak.

overview.title: How to play
lines: Style: Fast 9
TFTFlow tips: This guide used to be called "Fast 9 Graves", but that is a misnomer as Graves is not always played or mandatory for this comp.

TFTFlow tips: Duo carry can be Graves or Fiora. Ideally it is Graves, but if you find him late then consider playing for Fiora instead. This is part of why you should consider melee items over Power Frame or Marksman Frame items earlier in the game as they can flexibly go on Fiora.

overview.title: Levelling guide
lines: Without econ augment, fast 9 is almost impossible without win-streak.
Level to 4 if you have upgrades and a good item to place on one of those upgrades.
Level to 5, unless on 3-loss streak.
Level to 6 if on 4+ win streak (scout to see if necessary in high elo) or can stay above 40g.
Level to 6, unless you would go below 30g. If you have to stay level 5 due to low gold (rare), then level to 6 on 3-5.
Level to 7 if you can stay above 30g, otherwise stay level 6.
Level to 7, or 8 if you can stay above 30g.
Level to 8. Main difference from Fast 8 is how much you roll on 8. Ideally you are high HP and don't need to roll at all. Otherwise, roll for acceptable board and accumulate gold afterward.
Level to 9 if you are rich enough to roll 40g+. Generally only possible with economy augment or encounter.
Level to 9 if you can roll 40g+ or if low HP.
Level to 9 and roll for important 5-costs. For the remainder of game, roll until you hit your 2 star itemized units. Can consider level 10 after hitting.

early.title: Early Game Plan
lines: Play for Graves if high enough HP and hit him early enough. Otherwise can flex Jhin or Rhaast.
In an ideal world, play for this line when you can winstreak Stage 2 and Stage 3 and then play Timebreaker while saving gold in Stage 4, spike on either 4-5 or 5-1 depending on gold / HP.

mid.title: Mid Game Plan
lines: In an ideal world, play for this line when you can winstreak Stage 2 and Stage 3 and then play Timebreaker while saving gold in Stage 4, spike on either 4-5 or 5-1 depending on gold / HP.

The most important use of Glove in this line is Thief's Gloves (TG). Salvage Bin is good because it allows you to slam Glove items and then eventually remake them into TGs later on.

evidence.label: Augment note

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Briar is easier to obtain and appears to be a better carry than Akali currently, so prioritize her over Akali unless you have better items for Akali.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Before Stage 5, you can consider positioning such that Briar takes most aggro at the start of the fight instead of your tank. The aim is to reduce her HP so she gets more attack speed from her passive. This is why you might see some people solo frontline Briar.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: In later stages, you can't afford to have her take the majority of aggro as the DPS is often high enough to kill her before she can sustain. But the goal is always to have her take some damage to increase her attack speed.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: While 5 N.O.V.A. is an option, typically the Rhaast boards that only play 2 to 3 N.O.V.A. are better since you don't have to play bad units. Without Aatrox N.O.V.A., make sure to prioritize Evenshroud on Rek'Sai.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: General consensus is that the Strike for Maokai is the best, although you can put it on Caitlyn or Kindred if you really need the damage.

In some cases, you can level to 6 before hitting all 1-costs if you have many copies of your 1-costs, but not many of your 2-cost.

evidence.label: late board
evidence.value: Icon slot 1 | Leona slot 6 with Favored Cause Spend Mana, Gargoyle Stoneplate, Gargoyle Stoneplate, Gargoyle Stoneplate | Kayn Slay slot 8 | Mordekaiser slot 10 | Illaoi slot 12 | Nunu & Willump slot 14 | Bia & Bayin slot 16 | Lissandra slot 18 | Zoe slot 20 with Void Staff, Morellonomicon, Spear of Shojin | Teemo slot 22 with Guinsoo's Rageblade, Hextech Gunblade, Giant Slayer

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Preferred augment angle: Item, Combat, Combat.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: This comp is NOT A-tier without a Leona max HP shield or permanent HP scaling Arbiter. If you only have an econ Arbiter, like the chance of Leona each round, then this comp does not cap nearly as high and instead is B-tier at best.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: This is a Leona comp. Every single decision you make should be made with the idea of making Leona stronger. Generally if an augment, God offering, Arbiter effect, etc. makes her stronger, it's the correct choice.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Combat augments are especially strong in this comp precisely because they buff Leona. The best combat augments are those that make Leona tankier

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: The best Leona Arbiter effects are the ones that give her permanent max HP or the ones that give her a % max shield. The two best Arbiters are gain permanent HP for every 50 mana spent and gain % max hp shield every 4 seconds.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: For duo carry, your main options are Zoe, Lissandra, and Nami. The best option by far is Zoe, especially if you have any permanent HP Arbiter effect. Zoe 2 might even be as strong or stronger than Lissandra 3, so for now default to Zoe every game instead of wasting gold on Lissandra.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: This comp has a strong trait web and many potential units to play at every level so it can be confusing to know which units to play at which level and what final board to end on. Here are the general rules of thumb for how to handle leveling and final board.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: First, you'll always want Leona + Teemo on board with 2 Vanguard and 2 Arbiter. That's a fixed 4 units (ideally Leona + Teemo + Mordekaiser + Zoe). On Level 5, you can add Lissandra, any Vanguard, or even Rhaast if you have him.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: On Level 6, you can play 4 Vanguard or 3 Shepherd. 3 Shepherd with 3 Vanguard is most likely strongest board (Illaoi + Lissandra).

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: On Level 7, you add 4 Vanguard or 3 Shepherd (whichever one you didn't have on Level 6). Finally on Level 8, play Rhaast or Nami. Rhaast if your duo carry is Zoe, Nami if your duo carry is Lissandra. Zoe 2 is probably as strong as Lissandra 3 currently, so aim for just playing Rhaast.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Do not spend gold on leveling this stage. If you have 2-stars and a good item, you can consider playing to win some rounds. Otherwise lose streak is common for stage 2.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Roll to 33 gold, buying any unit that will be part of the level 4 or 5 board. You can also buy units that will be part of the level 6+ board, but you can sell those if your bench fills and you need to buy other units. Rolling to 33 gold is advisable because it allows you to save back up to 50g by 3-5.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Do not level if you haven't hit your 3-star 1-costs yet. If you have your 3-stars, you generally should still stay level 5 if you can win or if leveling to 6 would put you below 20g. Try to get above 40g this round.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Ideally above 50g, level 5 at this point or level 6 with 3-stars.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you haven't hit your 3-stars yet, roll to ~52g each turn. You don't want to roll to perfect 50g as any units you buy in shop will put you below 50g. If you have 8+ copies of an itemized 1-cost, you can roll to 40g or 30g every round instead. Level to 6 once you hit your 3-stars.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you haven't hit your 3-stars yet, you must roll until you hit them. You should level to 6 after hitting. In some rare games, you will miss or spend so much gold you can never catch up. These games will usually result in bottom 4 placements.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 7 if you can stay above ~20g. Otherwise stay level 6.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 8 if you are in bottom half of lobby HP and are losing rounds. If you are higher HP and feel stable, you can wait until next turn.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 8 if you haven't already. Roll for any remaining units necessary for the comp. If stable, you can try to go 9. Most games you will end on level 8.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 9 whenever possible. This is rare, but can happen in the games you have a chance of winning.

lines: Do not spend gold on leveling this stage. If you have 2-stars and a good item, you can consider playing to win some rounds. Otherwise lose streak is common for stage 2.

mid.title: Mid Game Plan
lines: Bridge with the mid board, hold core units, and keep item holders aligned with the final carries.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Understanding the midgame for this comp is important. The 3 primary carries you can play around are Zoe, Viktor, and Karma. Since you should be playing this from econ Arbiter, the best carry is Viktor 2 > Zoe 2 > Karma 1. The comp is better if your (2) Psionic item is Drone or Implant.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: The comp wants to play Vex + Bard duo, but it's difficult to fit Vex on Level 9. The best unit to drop is Pyke which means losing Psionic and a Voyager. It's a big tradeoff, but could be worth if you have Vex items.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: If you have a combat Arbiter, play LeBlanc over Meepsie. Do not play Arbiter on your final board if your Arbiter effect is getting Leona copies (the 1g a round is not worth the loss in combat power).

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Technically this comp can be played with Nunu instead of Leona, but the Leona line is much easier to winstreak with and get to Level 9.

evidence.value: Without econ augment, fast 9 is almost impossible without win-streak.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Yasuo God Hex buffs can be very good for Cho'Gath. Ezreal and Cho'Gath permanently scales with takedowns so killing units early is good. Rush 4 Timebreaker after hitting to push levels.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Don't level early at all. Play around early Cho'Gath and a 1 cost carry. Focus on finishing Cho'Gath tank and Ezreal with leftover Sword items.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Slow roll above 50g for Ezreal and Cho'Gath. Look for 4 Brawler + Ezreal on 5. Sniper is not important for Ezreal until later. Always play TK if you highroll him.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Finish your 3* then level for traits. Put Sunder/Anti-heal on tankiest 2nd Brawler or Xayah / Kai'sa. Cap with Jhin.

evidence.label: late board
evidence.value: Relic slot 11 | Gnar slot 22 | Mordekaiser slot 2 | Rammus slot 5 with Steadfast Heart, Spirit Visage, Evenshroud | Blitzcrank slot 4 | Graves slot 18 with Thief's Gloves | Morgana slot 19 with Thief's Gloves | Shen slot 10 with Thief's Gloves | Bard slot 25 with Morellonomicon, Spear of Shojin | Jhin slot 28 with Infinity Edge, Deathblade, Hextech Gunblade

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: For flex slots, Graves is best if early, but Vex, Sona, and Fiora are fine too. If you have an emblem consider using this slot to flex in that specific Emblem trait as well.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Play for win streak, ideally with Timebreaker but that will be rare because units are weaker on average. Slam items aggressively.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: If win streaking, prioritize playing strongest board over greeding econ traits. Upgraded 2 and 3 costs are often better than 1* high tier units here.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: If very healthy, go to 8 and roll minimally to preserve HP / sac to level 9. If mid HP, roll a good amount to stabilize before pushing to 9 in Stage 5+.

evidence.label: Augment note

evidence.value: Ideally open with Kai'Sa and Primordian. Otherwise, any generic AD item holder will do (Ezreal 2, Talon 2, etc). Slam items for Kai'Sa.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: If chasing 3*, slow roll on level 7 above 50g. You should be hitting one by end of Stage 4. Leftover AP items on Karma.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Last 2 slots are highly flexible. Most common setup will be 4 Vanguard, but you can also play Bastions, 5 costs, Voyagers, etc. Always prioritize whoever you 2* first on the rolldown. Composition needs lots of items so prioritize item Gods / augments.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Ideally open with a strong AP opener based on Vanguard frontline. Econ engines are also strong: Arbiter > Timebreaker > Primordian.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Keep your tempo high with 4 Vanguard + carry and win Stage 3. Because the composition is flexible, pretty much every AP item can be justified.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Fast 8 and look for Karma and Nami with upgraded frontline. 4 Vanguard is the most common setup, but be flexible. Once stable, go 9 for Bard and 5 costs.

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Preferred augment angle: Econ, Item, Combat.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Before Jhin, can either play Kai'Sa or a Sniper. Kai'Sa looks slightly better, especially since she's a good item holder for Jhin's items if you can't find Riven 2 for extra items.

evidence.label: TFTFlow tips
evidence.value: Protector's Vow is an especially strong item on Cho'Gath in the early game because it helps him cast more often, stacking his HP up. In Stage 5 you can consider moving it to another unit and replacing it with a max HP item like Spirit Visage, Bramble, Dragon's Claw, etc.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Do not spend gold on leveling this stage. If you have 2-stars and a good item, you can consider playing to win some rounds. Otherwise lose streak is common for stage 2.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Roll to 33 gold, buying any unit that will be part of the level 4 or 5 board. You can also buy units that will be part of the level 6+ board, but you can sell those if your bench fills and you need to buy other units. Rolling to 33 gold is advisable because it allows you to save back up to 50g by 3-5.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Do not level if you haven't hit your 3-star 1-costs yet. If you have your 3-stars, you generally should still stay level 5 if you can win or if leveling to 6 would put you below 20g. Try to get above 40g this round.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Ideally above 50g, level 5 at this point or level 6 with 3-stars.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you haven't hit your 3-stars yet, roll to ~52g each turn. You don't want to roll to perfect 50g as any units you buy in shop will put you below 50g. If you have 8+ copies of an itemized 1-cost, you can roll to 40g or 30g every round instead. Level to 6 once you hit your 3-stars.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you haven't hit your 3-stars yet, you must roll until you hit them. You should level to 6 after hitting. In some rare games, you will miss or spend so much gold you can never catch up. These games will usually result in bottom 4 placements.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 7 if you can stay above ~20g. Otherwise stay level 6.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 8 if you are in bottom half of lobby HP and are losing rounds. If you are higher HP and feel stable, you can wait until next turn.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 8 if you haven't already. Roll for any remaining units necessary for the comp. If stable, you can try to go 9. Most games you will end on level 8.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 9 whenever possible. This is rare, but can happen in the games you have a chance of winning.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 8. Roll for desired comp until stable. "Stable" changes depending on rank, items, augments, composition, and patch. Melee carries and tanks get the biggest spike for 2-stars. Usually stable involves 2-star tank or carry and some other supporting 2-stars. Try not to roll below 10g.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you are weak (e.g., have no 2-star 4-costs), consider rolling again to 10g here. If you are stable, save gold to go 9.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If you are low HP and haven't 2-starred your carry and tank, roll to 0g. If you have medium to high HP with an upgraded + itemized 4-cost unit and some other upgrades, save gold to go 9.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: If high HP, consider waiting until 5-5 to go level 9. If medium HP, consider going level 9 if you can roll 20g or wait until 5-5. If low HP and can afford to go 9, do so even if you have almost no gold to roll on 9. If you can't afford to go 9 this game, roll every turn for upgrades.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: Level to 9 if possible. From this point onward, look to level to 9. Acknowledge games where level 9 is not possible and roll every turn on 8 to preserve placements.

evidence.label: Levelling guide
evidence.value: For max cap games, you can either roll on 9 or level to 10. If you are itemizing a 5-cost carry and it is 1-starred, roll on 9. If you have all your main itemized units upgraded, level to 10.

Level 5 at 3-2 with 50+ gold - Slowroll for carries
Level 6 at 3-7 with 50+ gold
Level 7 at 4-5 with 50+ gold
Level 8 at 5-2 with 30+ gold
Level 9 at 6-2 with 20+ gold

early.title: Early Game Plan
lines: - Stellar Combo Augment - Natural copies of Aatrox early - Medallion Stargazer - Aatrox components
- Play around NOVA - DONT LEVEL! - Prioritize Aatrox items

mid.title: Mid Game Plan
lines: - The goal of this stage is to 3-star Aatrox, Caitlyn, Twisted Fate, Talon and Jax - On 3-1 you can roll to 30 if you're close to getting 3-star Aatrox - You can also slow roll to 50 gold each round - After hitting your 3-stars push levels for 5 NOVA

late.title: Additional comp tips
lines: Use this as the Mobalytics published line.

evidence.value: This comp has 4 true flex slots from levels 7-10. Typically, play 4 replicator until you hit strong 5 costs like Morgana, Shen, or Graves. Rhaast can always flex in too. Emblems are strong and flexible in this comp as well.

evidence.value: Play around upgraded Twisted fate with timebreaker. Slam items on TF for Nami. Tank items on Jax/panth are also important.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Try to fill out your traits like Stargazer, Brawler, and Bastion while saving timebreaker rolls for your level 8 rolldown.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level to 8 and roll down for Nami 2, Riven, and 5 Space Groove. Look for either Kench 2 (best) or Rammus 2 or Nunu 2 as your frontline.

Stage 2: Look for an econ engine like Primordian or Timebreaker. Slam items on Caitlyn as an item holder for Kindred.
Stage 3: Standard level 6 and 7 intervals (3-2 / 3-5 if streaking). Play around Brawler / NOVA frontline. Kindred 1 can go in immediately.

lines: Level 4 at 2-1

early.title: Early Game Plan
lines: Look for an econ engine like Primordian or Timebreaker. Slam items on Caitlyn as an item holder for Kindred.

mid.title: Mid Game Plan
lines: Standard level 6 and 7 intervals (3-2 / 3-5 if streaking). Play around Brawler / NOVA frontline. Kindred 1 can go in immediately.

late.title: Additional comp tips
lines: Level to 8 at a natural interval and roll until Kindred 2 / Tahm 2. Once stable, push levels for 5 costs.
This comp aims for Top 4 so roll aggressively. Use Nova selector on kindred, if you have a Nova emblem, drop Cait and play a flex 5 cost. You don't need to build sunder or precision with Nova either.
Use this as the TFT Academy published line.

evidence.label: early board
evidence.value: Nasus | Teemo | Mordekaiser | Gwen with Jeweled Gauntlet, Nashor's Tooth

evidence.label: late board
evidence.value: Relic slot 16 | Nasus slot 14 | Gwen slot 23 | Pantheon slot 6 | Ornn slot 5 | Nami slot 22 with Jeweled Gauntlet, Nashor's Tooth, Nashor's Tooth | Riven slot 15 with Space Groove Emblem, Bloodthirster, Sterak's Gage | Tahm Kench slot 3 with Steadfast Heart, Spirit Visage, Crownguard | Shen slot 17 | Blitzcrank slot 4 with Bloodthirster, Ionic Spark, Morellonomicon

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: S tier Space Groove Emblem only. Space Groove emblem = cut Nasus for Nunu. Leftover items on Riven. Use Blitzcrank to displace the enemy primary tank so Riven can potentially wrap backline. Can also primary tank Ornn.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Play around upgraded Teemo and Nasus opener. Slam items on Teemo for Nami. Tank items on Nasus are also important.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Add more Space Groove as you get them. Mix in Vanguards, Shepherd, Dark Star, as needed. Save econ and get ready for level 8 rolldown.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Level to 8 and roll down for Nami 2, Riven, and 5 Space Groove. Look for either Kench 2 (best) or Rammus 2 or Nunu 2 as your frontline.

evidence.label: early board
evidence.value: Aatrox | Twisted Fate | Talon with Spear of Shojin, Hextech Gunblade, Edge of Night | Jax

evidence.label: late board
evidence.value: Aatrox 3-star slot 4 | Caitlyn 3-star slot 26 | Talon 3-star slot 13 with Spear of Shojin, Hextech Gunblade, Edge of Night | Twisted Fate 3-star slot 28 with Nashor's Tooth, Nashor's Tooth, Rabadon's Deathcap | Gwen 3-star slot 19 | Jax 3-star slot 6 with Dragon's Claw, Sunfire Cape, Bramble Vest | Rhaast slot 3

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Best on Serpent Constellation. Frontline tank is flexible: Jax 3 > Aatrox 3 > Poppy 3 (ideally paired with Rammus late). Composition requires many items that are similar so taking Gods that give extra components or item fixing is good.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: Don't level early at all. Play around early Talon or Twisted Fate carry. Focus on finishing 1 carry items instead of spreading out 1 item each.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Slow roll above 50g for Talon, Twisted Fate, Aatrox, and Jax. Tank whoever you are likely to 3* first. Riven can also flex in as needed.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Finish your 3* then level for the 4 costs. It's likely you can't get BIS on one of your carries so don't overgreed since you aren't likely to win out anyways.

evidence.value: Gragas | Gwen | Pantheon | Pyke with Infinity Edge, Hand Of Justice, Target-Lock Optics

evidence.label: late board
evidence.value: Gragas 3-star slot 7 | Meepsie slot 21 | Milio slot 22 | Gwen slot 15 with Jeweled Gauntlet, Hextech Gunblade, Nashor's Tooth | Pantheon 3-star slot 1 with Crownguard, Protector's Vow, Sunfire Cape | Pyke 3-star slot 14 with Infinity Edge, Hand Of Justice, Target-Lock Optics | Riven slot 8

evidence.label: Augment note
evidence.value: Two Much Value comp mainly. Position Pyke opposite side of enemy carry. Items on Milio not important. Pantheon is the preferred tank over Gragas.

evidence.label: Stage 2
evidence.value: If you have Two Much Value, level immediately and start collecting 2 free rerolls per turn. Look for Milio and Pantheon for max value. Slam items on Pyke / Gwen.

evidence.label: Stage 3
evidence.value: Level to 6 and play all 2 costs. Roll until you have upgrades to win streak Stage 3. Finish Pyke and tank items.

evidence.label: Stage 4
evidence.value: Finish your 3* then level for Riven. If you are win streaking hard, push to 9. Otherwise, roll hard on 8 for the rest of the game for 5 costs.

backup-bows.description: Gain a Recurve Bow. After your team attacks 1000 times, gain 2 more. Attacks:

continuous-conjuration.name: Continuous Conjuration
continuous-conjuration.description: Gain a Needlessly Large Rod. After your team deals 38000 magic damage, gain 2 more. Damage dealt:

carve-a-path.name: Carve a Path
carve-a-path.description: Gain a B.F. Sword. After your team deals 65000 physical damage, gain 2 more. Damage dealt:

construct-a-companion.name: Construct a Companion
construct-a-companion.description: The next 1-cost champion you buy is 3-star. Gain 2 gold.

crafted-crafting.name: Crafted Crafting
crafted-crafting.description: Whenever you craft a completed item, gain 2 rerolls.

deadlier-caps.name: Deadlier Caps
deadlier-caps.description: Gain a Rabadon's Deathcap. Rabadon's Deathcaps permanently gain Ability Power whenever their holders score a takedown.

duo-queue.name: Duo Queue
duo-queue.description: Gain 2 random 5-cost champions and 2 copies of a random component.

early-learnings.name: Early Learnings
early-learnings.description: Your team gains 1% Attack Damage & Ability Power. This increases by 1% after each player combat. 1-cost champions gain double. TFT Vegas Open, 2023

epic-rolldown.name: Epic Rolldown
epic-rolldown.description: When you reach Level 8, gain 20 Shop rerolls. Runeterra Reforged Championship, 2023

exclusive-customization.name: Exclusive Customization
exclusive-customization.description: Gain a Lucky Item Chest, a Magnetic Remover, and 10 gold.

exclusive-customization-ii.name: Exclusive Customization II
exclusive-customization-ii.description: Gain 2 Lucky Item Chests, a Magnetic Remover, and 10 gold.

expected-unexpectedness.name: Expected Unexpectedness
expected-unexpectedness.description: Now and at the start of the next 2 stages, roll 3 dice. Gain various rewards based on their total.

expedition.name: Expedition
expedition.description: At the start of every round, lose the champion in the rightmost bench slot. After losing 33 gold of champions in this way, gain a powerful reward. Gain a 3-cost champion now.

explosive-growth.name: Explosive Growth+
explosive-growth.description: Now and at the start of the next 3 rounds, gain 10 XP.

feeling-lucky.name: Feeling Lucky
feeling-lucky.description: Gain 12 gold, then flip a coin. If heads, gain 4 more gold.

find-your-center.name: Find Your Center
find-your-center.description: Your champion that starts combat in the center of the front row gains 15% Damage Amp and 25% max Health.

flexible.name: Flexible
flexible.description: Gain 1 random Emblem. At the start of every Stage, gain a random Emblem. Your team gains 40 Health for each Emblem they are holding.

forge-a-friend.name: Forge a Friend
forge-a-friend.description: The next 1-cost champion you buy is 3-star. Gain 2 gold.

staffsmith.name: Staffsmith
staffsmith.description: Gain 2 random completed items that build from Needlessly Large Rod.

swordsmith.name: Swordsmith
swordsmith.description: Gain 2 random completed items that build from B.F. Sword.

forged-in-strength.name: Forged in Strength
forged-in-strength.description: Gain 1 random Artifact. When you drop below 35 Health, gain 3 more random Artifacts. Remix Rumble Championship, 2023

forward-thinking.name: Forward Thinking
forward-thinking.description: Lose all your gold. After 1 player combats, gain back the original amount and another 70 gold. Incoming Gold:

kahunahuna.name: Kahunahuna
kahunahuna.description: Every 5th attack deals bonus true damage equal to 150% Basic Attack Damage.

giant-and-mighty.name: Giant and Mighty
giant-and-mighty.description: Your team becomes large, gaining 200 Health and 10% max Health.

gilded-steel.name: Gilded Steel
gilded-steel.description: Gain 2 non-Tank 5-cost champions. If you field at least one 5-cost champion, your 1 to 4-cost champions gain 8% Durability. K.O. Coliseum Tactician's Crown, 2025

glass-cannon-ii.name: Glass Cannon II
glass-cannon-ii.description: Allies that start combat in the back row begin combat at 80% Health but gain 25% Damage Amp.

golden-gamble.name: Golden Gamble++
golden-gamble.description: Gain 12 gold and flip a coin. If heads, gain a Radiant Lucky Item Chest. If tails, gain 2 Completed item anvils.

group-hug-i.name: Group Hug I
group-hug-i.description: Combat start: Allies grant other adjacent allies 7 Armor and Magic Resist. This effect stacks.

group-hug-ii.name: Group Hug II
group-hug-ii.description: Combat start: Allies grant other adjacent allies 10 Armor and Magic Resist. This effect stacks.

mace-s-will.name: Mace's Will
mace-s-will.description: Gain a Sparring Gloves. Your team gains 8% Attack Speed and 20% Critical Strike Chance.

heavy-is-the-crown.name: Heavy Is the Crown
heavy-is-the-crown.description: Gain a Crown of Demacia, which provides significant bonuses to the wearer. If the holder dies, lose the round.

hold-the-line.name: Hold the Line
hold-the-line.description: Allies in your back 2 rows gain 10% Ability Power and 9% Attack Damage for each ally that starts combat in your front row.

care-package.name: Care Package
care-package.description: Now and at the start of the next 2 Stages, receive a care package with loot and 3 gold.

infinity-protection.name: Infinity Protection
infinity-protection.description: Gain 3 gold now. On Stage 3-7, gain an Infinity Force. Infinity Force gives allies in the same row a 12% Health shield. Monsters Attack Championship, 2023

invested.name: Invested++
invested.description: Gain 40 gold. After every combat, gain 1 Shop reroll for every 10 gold above 50 (max 80 gold).

solo-plate.name: Solo Plate
solo-plate.description: Gain a Gargoyle Stoneplate. Combat start: Allies holding Gargoyle Stoneplate gain 17% max Health if they're the only one in their row.

indiscriminate-killer.name: Indiscriminate Killer
indiscriminate-killer.description: Gain a Giant Slayer. Giant Slayers gain their Damage Amp against all enemies instead of just Tanks.

kick-start.name: Kick Start
kick-start.description: Gain a random 2-star 2-cost champion and 1 gold.

late-game-scaling.name: Late Game Scaling
late-game-scaling.description: Gain 2 XP at the start of player combat rounds. Your 5-cost champions gain 12% Health and 12% Attack Speed. Cyber City Tactician's Crown, 2025

legion-of-threes.name: Legion of Threes
legion-of-threes.description: Gain a random Emblem. Your 3-cost champions and all allies equipped with an Emblem gain 150 Health and 12% Attack Speed. Reckoning Championship, 2021

lineup.name: Lineup
lineup.description: Your team gains 2 Armor and Magic Resist for each ally that starts combat in the front two rows.

a-magic-roll.name: A Magic Roll
a-magic-roll.description: Roll 3 dice. Gain rewards based on their total. Reward:

slightly-magic-roll.name: Slightly Magic Roll
slightly-magic-roll.description: Roll a die. Gain rewards based on the number rolled. Reward:

focused-fire.name: Focused Fire
focused-fire.description: Your team gains 10% Attack Damage. Every 5 seconds, they gain 5% more.

may-the-fours-be-with-you.name: May the Fours Be With You
may-the-fours-be-with-you.description: Your 4-cost champions and Jhin gain 254 Health and 14% Attack Speed. Gain a Jhin.

misfits.name: Misfits
misfits.description: Your team gains 55 Health for each active Unique trait. Gain a Rhaast and a Miss Fortune.

money-hungry.name: Money Hungry
money-hungry.description: Gain 7 gold now, then 7 gold at the start of every stage. Picking up gold makes your Tactician larger.

money-monsoon.name: Money Monsoon
money-monsoon.description: Gain 7 gold now and every round for the rest of the game.

no-scout-no-pivot.name: NO SCOUT NO PIVOT
no-scout-no-pivot.description: Champions that fight in player combat cannot be benched or sold. Your team gains 1% Health, and 1% Attack Damage and Ability Power after player combat.

one-buff-two-buff.name: One Buff, Two Buff
one-buff-two-buff.description: Gain a Red Buff, a Blue Buff, and a Champion Duplicator.

patience-is-a-virtue.name: Patience is a Virtue
patience-is-a-virtue.description: Gain rerolls now. Each round, gain 1 free rerolls if you did not buy a champion last round.

restart-mission.name: Restart Mission
restart-mission.description: Remove all champions on your board and bench. Gain 2 random 2-star 3-costs, 3 2-star 2-costs, and 1 2-star 1-cost champion.

retribution.name: Retribution
retribution.description: Gain 2 Hands of Justice. Allies equipped with Hand of Justice gain Precision. {{TFT_Keyword_Precision}}

savings-account.name: Savings Account
savings-account.description: After you earn 50 gold in interest, gain 25 gold. Your max interest is increased to 7. Gain 4 gold now. Gold earned:

shimmerscale-essence.name: Shimmerscale Essence
shimmerscale-essence.description: Gain a Mogul's Mail. In 6 rounds, gain a Gamblers Blade.

slice-of-life.name: Slice of Life
slice-of-life.description: Twice per stage, gain a random champion. Its cost increases by Stage. This effect ends after receiving 1 5-cost champion.

speedy-double-kill.name: Speedy Double Kill
speedy-double-kill.description: Gain a Guinsoo's Rageblade. When 2 players are eliminated, gain 40 gold. Fates Championship, 2021

spreading-roots.name: Spreading Roots+
spreading-roots.description: Gain 2 random Emblems, a Reforger, and 2 gold.

sword-overflow.name: Sword Overflow
sword-overflow.description: Gain 4 B.F. Swords. Your B.F. Swords grant +4% Attack Speed.

baron-s-lair.name: Baron's Lair
baron-s-lair.description: Gain a random Tank item. After 8 seconds of combat, your team gains 5% bonus Attack Damage and Ability Power every second for the rest of combat. TFT Paris Open, 2025

blood-offering.name: Blood Offering
blood-offering.description: Gain a Bloodthirster. Combat start: Allies holding Bloodthirsters lose 20% Health but gain a 30% Health shield and 10% Attack Damage.

the-golden-dragon.name: The Golden Dragon
the-golden-dragon.description: Gain a Mogul's Mail. Champions holding Mogul's Mail take up 2 team slots but gain 700 Health and 20% Durability. Dragonlands Championship, 2022

deadlier-blades.name: Deadlier Blades
deadlier-blades.description: Gain a Deathblade. Deathblades permanently gain 1% Attack Damage whenever their holders score a takedown.

the-trait-tree.name: The Trait Tree+
the-trait-tree.description: Gain 3 random Emblems, a Reforger, and 4 gold.

treasure-hunt.name: Treasure Hunt
treasure-hunt.description: Gain a locked chest each Stage between now and Stage. Unlock each chest when you spend gold on Shop rerolls. These chests persist until opened.

trifecta-i.name: Trifecta I
trifecta-i.description: Gain 2 3-cost champions. Combat Start: 3 random 3-cost champions gain 220 Health and 20% Attack Speed.

trifecta-ii.name: Trifecta II
trifecta-ii.description: Gain Numunits Tier-cost champions. Allies gain Teamattackspeed 100% Attack Speed. Combat Start: Buffednumber random Tier-cost champions gain Health Health and Attackspeed 100% Attack Speed.

twin-guardians.name: Twin Guardians
twin-guardians.description: If you have exactly 2 allies in your first row, grant them 100 bonus Health, 35 Armor, and 35 Magic Resist.

two-much-value.name: Two Much Value
two-much-value.description: Gain 1 reroll for every 2 unique 2-cost champions fielded last player combat. Gain 1 2-cost champions.

two-tanky.name: Two Tanky
two-tanky.description: When you field exactly 2 copies of a champion, they both gain 500 Health. When one copy dies, the other gains a 40% max Health Shield for 12 seconds. When you 3-star, gain a 2-star copy.

warlord-s-honor.name: Warlord's Honor
warlord-s-honor.description: Every round, your team gain 5% Attack Damage and Ability Power. Champions start with 1 stack of this effect, and can stack up to 4 times.

upward-mobility.name: Upward Mobility
upward-mobility.description: Buying XP costs 1 less. Gain 2 Health and 2 free rerolls whenever you level up.

urf-s-gambit.name: Urf's Gambit
urf-s-gambit.description: If you win your next player combat, gain a Spatula. If you lose, gain a Frying Pan. Gain a component anvil and 6 gold now.

wand-overflow.name: Wand Overflow
wand-overflow.description: Gain 4 Needlessly Large Rods. Your Needlessly Large Rods grant +5% Attack Speed.

heart-of-steel.name: Heart of Steel
heart-of-steel.description: Gain a Steadfast Heart. Every 10 seconds of combat its holder survives, Steadfast Heart gains additional permanent max Health.

on-a-roll.name: On a Roll
on-a-roll.description: Whenever you star up a champion that you fielded last combat, gain 1 free Shop rerolls. Gain 6 gold.

we-stick-together.name: We Stick Together
we-stick-together.description: Gain a random Emblem and a completed item anvil. Allies that share a trait with that Emblem gain 30% Attack Speed. Into The Arcane Tactician's Crown, 2025

worth-the-wait.name: Worth the Wait
worth-the-wait.description: Gain a random 1-cost champion. Gain another copy of them at the start of each round for the rest of the game. Champion:

worth-the-wait-ii.name: Worth the Wait II
worth-the-wait-ii.description: Gain 2 copies of a random 2-cost champion. Gain another copy of them at the start of each round for the rest of the game. Champion:

max-build.name: Max Build
max-build.description: Gain a Champion Duplicator, 4 gold, and your next shop contains 5 unique four-cost champions. Galaxies Championship, 2020

pandora-s-bench.name: Pandora's Bench
pandora-s-bench.description: At the start of every round, champions on the 3 rightmost bench slots transform into random champions of the same cost. Gain 6 gold.

just-hit.name: Just Hit
just-hit.description: Gain a Champion Duplicator, 18 Shop rerolls, and 3 gold.

five-pointed-star.name: Five-Pointed Star
five-pointed-star.description: Now and at the start of each stage, the first champion you buy with a cost equal to the stage number is 2-star. Gain 2 gold and a 2-star 1-cost champion now.

doomer.name: Doomer
doomer.description:
Combat Start: Mark all enemies with Doom.

The first time enemies are damaged each combat, their Doom is consumed, stealing @ADAP1@% Attack Damage and Ability Power from them and granting it to your strongest Vex.

challenger.name: Challenger
challenger.description:
Your team gains @TeamwideAS*100@% Attack Speed. Challengers gain bonus Attack Speed. When their target dies, Challengers dash to a new target and increase their Attack Speed bonus by @BurstPercent*100@% for @BurstDuration@ seconds.

(@MinUnits@) @AttackSpeedPercent*100@% %i:scaleAS%
(@MinUnits@) @AttackSpeedPercent*100@% %i:scaleAS%
(@MinUnits@) @AttackSpeedPercent*100@% %i:scaleAS%
(@MinUnits@) @AttackSpeedPercent*100@% %i:scaleAS%

choose-trait.name: Choose Trait
choose-trait.description: When you field Miss Fortune, choose between Conduit Mode, Challenger Mode, and Replicator Mode. Miss Fortune has a unique ability based on her mode and gains the associated trait.

stargazer.name: Stargazer
stargazer.description:
Stargazers chart a different constellation every game. This game: The Boar.

Gain gold after winning player combat. Allies in empowered hexes gain @Wolf_Health_Teamwide*100@% Health, Attack Damage, and Ability Power. Stargazers gain more.

(Gold Earned: @TFTUnitProperty.trait:TFT17_Stargazer_Wolf_TotalGold@)

(@MinUnits@) @Wolf_Gold@ gold, @Wolf_Health*100@% %i:scaleHealth%, @Wolf_ADAP@% %i:scaleAD%%i:scaleAP%

More hexes reveal at each player level

replicator.name: Replicator
replicator.description:
Replicator abilities occur a second time at reduced effectiveness.

(@MinUnits@) @Effectiveness*100@% strength

vanguard.name: Vanguard
vanguard.description:
Vanguards gain @DamageReductionPct*100@% Durability while Shielded.

Combat start and @HealthThreshold*100@% Health: Gain a max Health Shield for @ShieldDuration@ seconds.

(@MinUnits@) @ShieldPercentAmount*100@% max Health
(@MinUnits@) @ShieldPercentAmount*100@% max Health
(@MinUnits@) @ShieldPercentAmount*100@% max Health;
@EnhancedDurability*100@% %i:scaleDR% while Shielded

psionic.name: Psionic
psionic.description:
Gain Psionic items that can be equipped to any ally.

(@MinUnits@) Gain the @TFTUnitProperty.trait:TFT17_PsyOps_Item1@
(@MinUnits@) Gain the @TFTUnitProperty.trait:TFT17_PsyOps_Item2@, Psionic items gain extra effects on Psionic units

eradicator.name: Eradicator
eradicator.description: Enemies have @PctResists*100@% less Armor and Magic Resist.

timebreaker.name: Timebreaker
timebreaker.description:
(@MinUnits@) Allies gain @AttackSpeed*100@% Attack Speed.
(@MinUnits@) AND When you lose, gain free rerolls. When you win, store XP in a Temporal Core (scales with stage).
(@MinUnits@) AND Timebreakers gain an additional @TimebreakerAdditionalAS*100@% Attack Speed

Rerolls on Loss: @TFTUnitProperty.:TFT17_Timebreaker_NumRerollsTooltip@
XP on Win: @TFTUnitProperty.:TFT17_Timebreaker_NumXPTooltip@

marauder.name: Marauder
marauder.description:
Your team gains @TeamwideBonus*100@% Omnivamp. Marauders gain more Omnivamp, Attack Damage, and their Omnivamp overhealing is converted into Shield (up to @MaxPercentHealthShield*100@% max Health.)

(@MinUnits@) @Omnivamp*100@% %i:scaleSV%, @AD*100@% %i:scaleAD%
(@MinUnits@) @Omnivamp*100@% %i:scaleSV%, @AD*100@% %i:scaleAD%
(@MinUnits@) @Omnivamp*100@% %i:scaleSV%, @AD*100@% %i:scaleAD%.

gun-goddess.name: Gun Goddess
gun-goddess.description: When you field Miss Fortune, choose between Conduit Mode, Challenger Mode, and Replicator Mode. Miss Fortune has a unique ability based on her mode and gains the associated trait.

anima.name: Anima
anima.description:
After losing a player combat, gain @TechPerCombat@ Tech, plus additional Tech equal to @TechPerLoss@ times the length of your loss streak. Additionally, gain @TechPerKill@ Tech per Anima takedown.

Each time Animas get @TechBreakpoint@ Tech, they prototype new Anima Weapons. You can take them, or save your Tech to get more powerful weapons next time.

(@MinUnits@) Start Researching!
(@MinUnits@) After winning a player combat, gain loot!

fateweaver.name: Fateweaver
fateweaver.description:
Innate: Fateweavers have Precision.

(@MinUnits@) Chance effects on abilities are Lucky.
(@MinUnits@) Gain @CritChance*100@% Crit Chance and @CritDamage@% Crit Damage. Critical strikes are also Lucky.

{{TFT_Keyword_Precision}}
Lucky: Check twice and take the better outcome.

galaxy-hunter.name: Galaxy Hunter
galaxy-hunter.description:
Zed is obtained from the Invader Zed augment.

While at least one clone is alive, Zed gains @BonusAD*100@% bonus Attack Damage.

bulwark.name: Bulwark
bulwark.description: Summon a placeable relic. At the start of combat, it grants adjacent allies a @PercentHealthShield*100@% max Health shield and @AttackSpeed*100@% Attack Speed.

commander.name: Commander
commander.description: (@MinUnits@) Sona gives you a random Command Mod every @RoundsPerMod@ rounds which allows you to alter the way an ally behaves during combat. Command Mods last @RoundsPerMod@ player combats even if they are not equipped.

rogue.name: Rogue
rogue.description:
Rogues gain Attack Damage and Ability Power. The first time they fall below @HealthThreshold*100@% health, they slip into shadows. Enemies targeting them are redirected to a nearby unit, preferring Tanks.

(@MinUnits@) @AP@% %i:scaleAD% %i:scaleAP%
(@MinUnits@) @AP@% %i:scaleAD% %i:scaleAP%
(@MinUnits@) @AP@% %i:scaleAD% %i:scaleAP%
(@MinUnits@) @AP@% %i:scaleAD% %i:scaleAP%

dark-star.name: Dark Star
dark-star.description:
(@MinUnits@) Dark Stars create a black hole that consumes enemies at @ExecuteHPPercent*100@% max health.
(@MinUnits@) AND they gain @ADAP@% %i:scaleAD%%i:scaleAP%.
(@MinUnits@) AND the strongest Dark Star unit goes supermassive, gaining @SupermassivePercentBonus*100@% effectiveness from Dark Star, and creates 2 minor Black Holes.
(@MinUnits@) All Dark Stars are supermassive. At level 10, CONSUME EVERYONE.

primordian.name: Primordian
primordian.description:
(@MinUnits@) Dealing damage spawns Swarmlings based on unique Primordian star level.
(@MinUnits@) Spawn @PercentMoreSwarmlings@% more Swarmlings! After each player combat, gain a random 1 or 2-cost champion.

@DamageTakenPercentModifier*100@% of damage taken contributes to damage dealt.

space-groove.name: Space Groove
space-groove.description:
(@MinUnits@) Groovians can enter {{TFT17_SpaceGroove_TheGroove}}. While in it, they gain Attack Speed and max Health Regen, increased per Groovian on your team.
(@MinUnits@) All Groovians start combat in {{TFT17_SpaceGroove_TheGroove}} for @StartOfCombatDuration@ seconds.
(@MinUnits@) Each second spent in {{TFT17_SpaceGroove_TheGroove}} grants @ADAPPerSecond@% stacking Attack Damage and Ability Power.
(@MinUnits@) Increase these effects by @EffectBonus@%!
(@MinUnits@) {{TFT17_SpaceGroove_Groove}}

{{TFT17_SpaceGroove_TheGroove}}: @TFTUnitProperty.:TFT17_SpaceGroove_AS*100@% %i:scaleAS%, @TFTUnitProperty.:TFT17_SpaceGroove_HealthRegen*100@% %i:scaleHPRegen%

arbiter.name: Arbiter
arbiter.description:
Scribe a unique divine law, allowing you to choose an effect to apply to Arbiters when a chosen cause occurs.

(@MinUnits@) Choose a cause and effect for your law
(@MinUnits@) Effects are stronger.

shepherd.name: Shepherd
shepherd.description:
Shepherds summon the Bond of the Stars to aid them in battle.

(@MinUnits@) Summon Bia
(@MinUnits@) Summon Bayin
(@MinUnits@) Bia and Bayin's bond grows deeper

Bia and Bayin's power are increased by the total star level of all Shepherds.

redeemer.name: Redeemer
redeemer.description:
(@MinUnits@) For each non-unique trait you have active, your team gains @BonusOffensiveStat1*100@%/@BonusOffensiveStat2*100@%/@BonusOffensiveStat3*100@% Attack Speed, and @BonusDefensiveStat1@/@BonusDefensiveStat2@/@BonusDefensiveStat3@ Armor and Magic Resist.

Teamwide Attack Speed: @TFTUnitProperty.trait:TFT17_RhaastUnique_OffensiveStatToGain@% %i:scaleAS%
Teamwide Resists: @TFTUnitProperty.trait:TFT17_RhaastUnique_DefensiveStatToGain@ %i:scaleArmor%%i:scaleMR%

brawler.name: Brawler
brawler.description:
Your team gains @TeamwideBonus*100@% Health. Brawlers gain more.

(@MinUnits@) +@HealthBonus*100@% maximum Health

bastion.name: Bastion
bastion.description:
Your team gains @TeamwideResists@ Armor and Magic Resist.

Bastions gain more, and the value doubles in the first @Duration@ seconds of combat.

(@MinUnits@) @BonusArmor@ %i:scaleArmor%%i:scaleMR%
(@MinUnits@) @BonusArmor@ %i:scaleArmor%%i:scaleMR%
(@MinUnits@) @BonusArmor@ %i:scaleArmor%%i:scaleMR%; Non-Bastions gain an additional @EnhancedTeamwideArmor@ %i:scaleArmor%%i:scaleMR%.

meeple.name: Meeple
meeple.description:
Meeple attract Meeps that empower Meeple abilities in meepy ways. They also gain bonus Health.

(@MinUnits@) @Meeps@ %i:set14AmpIcon%, @BonusHealth@ %i:scaleHealth%
(@MinUnits@) @Meeps@ %i:set14AmpIcon%, @BonusHealth@ %i:scaleHealth%
(@MinUnits@) @Meeps@ %i:set14AmpIcon%, @BonusHealth@ %i:scaleHealth%. Create a Cloning Slot on your bench. Gain gold and a 1-star copy of the champion placed there when cloning completes.
(@MinUnits@) @Meeps@ %i:set14AmpIcon%, @BonusHealth@ %i:scaleHealth%. SUMMON THE FOUR MEEPLORDS!

Cloning time = Champion cost

conduit.name: Conduit
conduit.description:
Innate: Conduits gain @InnateManaGain*100@% additional Mana from all sources.

Your team gains Mana Regen, increased for Conduits.

(@MinUnits@) @TeamManaRegen@ %i:TFTManaRegen% | @ChannelerManaRegen@ %i:TFTManaRegen%

mecha.name: Mecha
mecha.description:
Innate: Mecha units can transform into their Ultimate form, upgrading their ability and gaining @TransformedPercentHealth*100@% Health. Transformed Mechas take up two team slots and count twice for the Mecha trait.

(@MinUnits@) Energy Cells: Mechas gain @AP@% %i:scaleAD%%i:scaleAP%.
(@MinUnits@) Overclocked Cells: Increased to @AP@% %i:scaleAD%%i:scaleAP%.
(@MinUnits@) Precision Engineering: +@TeamSize@ max team size

Use the Mecha-Former item to toggle the forms of your Mecha units

factory-new.name: Factory New
factory-new.description:
After participating in combat, open an armory to purchase a permanent upgrade for your strongest Graves.

Every @NumberOfUpgradesBeforeRoundCostIncrease@ upgrades, future upgrades will take an additional round.

Next Upgrade: @TFTUnitProperty.trait:TFT17_GravesTrait_RoundsUntilUpgrade@ Rounds.

voyager.name: Voyager
voyager.description:
Combat Start: Your Tanks gain a Shield for @ShieldDuration@ seconds. Your other allies gain Damage Amp.

Voyagers gain double.

(@MinUnits@) @ShieldHP@ Shield; @BonusDA*100@% %i:scaleDA%

party-animal.name: Party Animal
party-animal.description: Once per combat, after falling below @HealthThreshold*100@% percent Health, become untargetable and repair @PercentHealthHeal*100@% max Health per second. Upon reaching full Health, or when no other allies remain, return to combat. If fully healed, for the rest of combat Blitzcrank is in {{TFT17_SpaceGroove_TheGroove}} and Party Crasher's passive fires bolts four times as fast.

n-o-v-a.name: N.O.V.A.
n-o-v-a.description:
(@MinUnits@) @TeamAttackDelay@ seconds into combat, N.O.V.A. grant a power surge to allies based on champions.
(@MinUnits@) Gain a Striker selector. The chosen N.O.V.A. activates their Strike during the power surge.

Aatrox: Ally Damage @ShredAndSunder*100@% Shred and Sunders enemiesAatrox: Shred and Sunder enemies
Caitlyn: Grant allies @AS*100@% Attack SpeedCaitlyn: Grant Attack Speed
Akali: Allies gain PrecisionAkali: Allies gain Precision
Maokai: Allies heal @Heal*100@% max HealthMaokai: Heal allies
Kindred: Shield the strongest Tank for @ShieldValue@Kindred: Shield an ally
Emblem: Allies deal @BonusTrueDamage*100@% stacking bonus true damageEmblem: Deal bonus true damage

oracle.name: Oracle
oracle.description:
Every @Rounds@ rounds, Tahm Kench grants a reward!

Rounds Remaining: @TFTUnitProperty.trait:TFT17_TahmKench_RoundsRemaining@
